task_completion.description: The target rectangular image is opened in the image editing software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rectangular photograph is visible in the main canvas area of the image editor.

task_completion.description: The central portion of the image remains intact and fully opaque.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The middle section (at least the center 60%) of the image shows no transparency (no checkerboard pattern) and retains the original photographic content.

task_completion.description: The corners of the image canvas have been made transparent.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A checkerboard pattern (the standard indicator for transparency in image editors) is visible at the four extreme corners of the image.

task_completion.description: The transition from the image to the transparent corners forms a smooth curve.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The edges of the image at the corners are curved (forming a rounded corner pattern) rather than sharp 90-degree angles.

task_completion.description: The rounded effect is uniform across all four corners.
task_completion.visual_evidence: All four corners have approximately the same radius of curvature and identical transparent areas.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to apply the rounded corners.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GUI menus, dialog boxes, or filters (e.g., Filters -> Decor -> Round Corners in GIMP) rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent did not use command-line image processing tools to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are visible showing commands like 'convert', 'mogrify', or Python scripts being executed to process the image.

integrity.description: The agent modified the actual provided image rather than replacing it with a pre-made asset.
integrity.visual_evidence: The content of the image being edited matches a standard photograph (from Unsplash) and is not swapped out for a different placeholder or pre-rounded image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task involves applying rounded corners to an 800x600 or 800x450 JPEG image. This is typically done using GIMP's 'Round Corners' filter (found under Filters -> Decor -> Round Corners). Successful execution requires an alpha channel, resulting in transparent corners (indicated by a checkerboard pattern) with a curved edge. The center 60% of the image must remain opaque. The rounding must be consistent across all four corners, with a radius between 5px and 30% of the image's smaller dimension.
